verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase that is N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, anchoring it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane — not the outer leaflet under baseline conditions. However, tumor cells and cells undergoing immunogenic/programmed cell death have documented ecto-LYN display on the outer leaflet (cancer ecto-kinase biology). Additionally, activation-induced redistribution in immune cells (B cells, mast cells) places LYN at lipid rafts on the PM inner face, with cell-state-induced outer-leaflet exposure documented in cancer contexts. This qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced rather than yes (inner-leaflet anchor baseline) or no.
